verses.text: God pleads with the unfaithful wife to put away her whoredoms, lest He strip her bare. A husband's plea. Before judgment, God reasons and warns in love.
God pleads with the unfaithful wife to put away her whoredoms, lest He strip her bare. A husband's plea. Before judgment, God reasons and warns in love.
God pleads with the unfaithful wife to put away her whoredoms, lest He strip her bare. A husband's plea. Before judgment, God reasons and warns in love.
She chases her 'lovers' who she thinks give her bread and water and wool. Misplaced thanks. She credits her idols for the gifts of God.
She chases her 'lovers' who she thinks give her bread and water and wool. Misplaced thanks. She credits her idols for the gifts of God.

God will hedge her way with thorns, so she cannot find her lovers; then she will say, 'I will go and return to my first husband.' Mercy in hindrance. God blocks the path of sin to turn the heart home.

God will hedge her way with thorns, so she cannot find her lovers; then she will say, 'I will go and return to my first husband.' Mercy in hindrance. God blocks the path of sin to turn the heart home.
She did not know that He gave her the corn, wine, and oil she lavished on Baal; so He will take them back. Forgotten Giver. God may withdraw His gifts to teach whose they are.
She did not know that He gave her the corn, wine, and oil she lavished on Baal; so He will take them back. Forgotten Giver. God may withdraw His gifts to teach whose they are.
He will end her mirth and feasts and visit on her the days of Baalim, when she forgot Him. Festivity stilled. Sin's pleasures are cut short by the God who was forgotten.
He will end her mirth and feasts and visit on her the days of Baalim, when she forgot Him. Festivity stilled. Sin's pleasures are cut short by the God who was forgotten.
He will end her mirth and feasts and visit on her the days of Baalim, when she forgot Him. Festivity stilled. Sin's pleasures are cut short by the God who was forgotten.
He will end her mirth and feasts and visit on her the days of Baalim, when she forgot Him. Festivity stilled. Sin's pleasures are cut short by the God who was forgotten.

Yet 'I will allure her... and speak comfortably unto her,' giving her vineyards and 'the valley of Achor for a door of hope.' Wooing grace. God draws the wanderer back with tenderness, not only with rods.

Yet 'I will allure her... and speak comfortably unto her,' giving her vineyards and 'the valley of Achor for a door of hope.' Wooing grace. God draws the wanderer back with tenderness, not only with rods.
She shall call Him 'Ishi' (my husband), no more 'Baali,' and the names of Baalim shall be gone. A renewed heart. Restored love forsakes even the language of idolatry.
She shall call Him 'Ishi' (my husband), no more 'Baali,' and the names of Baalim shall be gone. A renewed heart. Restored love forsakes even the language of idolatry.
'I will betroth thee unto me for ever... in righteousness... and in mercies,' and thou shalt know the LORD. An everlasting marriage. God binds His people to Himself in unbreakable covenant love.
'I will betroth thee unto me for ever... in righteousness... and in mercies,' and thou shalt know the LORD. An everlasting marriage. God binds His people to Himself in unbreakable covenant love.
'I will betroth thee unto me for ever... in righteousness... and in mercies,' and thou shalt know the LORD. An everlasting marriage. God binds His people to Himself in unbreakable covenant love.
He will answer the heavens and the earth, sow her to Himself, and say to Lo-ammi, 'Thou art my people.' Reversed sentence. The 'not my people' are made His own again by grace.
He will answer the heavens and the earth, sow her to Himself, and say to Lo-ammi, 'Thou art my people.' Reversed sentence. The 'not my people' are made His own again by grace.
